sanskrit:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसंमोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च।।
transliteration:
buddhir jñānam asammohaḥ kṣamā satyaṁ damaḥ śamaḥ
sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ bhavo ’bhāvo bhayaṁ cābhayam eva ca
literal_meaning: Intelligence, knowledge, freedom-from-delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, sense-control, mind-control, happiness, distress, birth, non-existence, fear, fearlessness—(all) indeed (come) from Me alone.
contextual_meaning: Every cognitive, emotional, and existential polarity—clarity vs. confusion, pleasure vs. pain, being vs. non-being, safety vs. threat—originates from the same divine field.  They are not private possessions but transient modalities of one Consciousness appearing as many minds.
psychological_state: Cognitive defusion: the listener is invited to observe every mental content—virtue or vice, comfort or discomfort—as an impersonal wave in the ocean of awareness, reducing self-judgment and increasing existential safety.
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy catastrophizes: 'If I feel anxious, something must be wrong with me.'  The therapist reflects: 'What if this anxiety, like every state, is a temporary face of the same field that also gifts calm?  Can we watch it together without the extra story?'
anchor_text: Every thought and feeling is a wave of one ocean; watch, don't claim.
